mcp.webview.title: VirtualTabs MCP Server Configuration
mcp.webview.tipBody: You can add this configuration to your AI IDE or assistant to let it automatically organize your files and groups.
mcp.webview.skillBody: While the MCP server provides tools, the AI Agent needs a 'Manual' to know when and how to use them effectively. Use the command `VirtualTabs: Generate Agent Skill` from the command palette.
mcp.webview.concurrencyNote: 🔒 Concurrency Safe: File locking is implemented to allow multiple AI agents to safely access VirtualTabs configuration simultaneously.
mcp.webview.workspaceDescription: Select which workspace path should be used for absolute-path MCP configurations.
mcp.webview.noWorkspaceWarning: No workspace detected. Open a folder/workspace before copying absolute-path configurations.
mcp.tool.cursor.name: Cursor
mcp.tool.cursor.instruction: Cursor Settings > Features > MCP > Add new MCP server
mcp.tool.copilot.name: GitHub Copilot
mcp.tool.copilot.instruction: Open GitHub Copilot MCP/server settings and add the VirtualTabs server configuration
mcp.tool.kiro.name: Kiro IDE
mcp.tool.kiro.instruction: Ctrl+Shift+P (Cmd+Shift+P on Mac) > 'Open workspace MCP config'
mcp.tool.claudeDesktop.name: Claude Code
mcp.tool.claudeDesktop.instruction: Add the configuration to Claude Code's configuration file, or run: claude mcp add virtualtabs <path>
mcp.tool.claudeDesktop.note:
Configuration file location:
macOS / Linux: ~/.claude.json
Windows: %USERPROFILE%\.claude.json
mcp.tool.antigravity.name: Antigravity
mcp.tool.antigravity.instruction: Add MCP Server in Antigravity Settings panel
